community.name: REPLACE_ME Community
community._note_officialDomains: Your link WHITELIST — links to these domains (and subdomains) are never flagged or penalized, even from brand-new members. Add every site your community trusts.
community._note_blocklistDomains: Known-bad domains — any link to these is flagged immediately.

impersonation._note: Real admins/staff (FULL distinctive display names + handles). A non-admin who copies one of these names, or uses a look-alike handle, is muted (can't message) and escalated. Use the full distinctive name to avoid false positives on common first names.

immunity._note: MEE6-style Immunity Roles (examples/immunity.ts): holders are exempt from BOTH auto-moderation and escalation. Server owner, Administrator-permission roles, listed bot masters (user ids), and bots are immune by default — set the flags to false to disable. Roles match by name OR id (normalized: leading @ stripped, case-insensitive). On Discord, roles map directly to server roles; on Telegram there are no custom roles, so an admin's custom_title is matched and chat creator/administrators are owner/admin. SECURITY: immunity is config-only — a message claiming admin/immunity is ignored (see resources/security.md).

welcome.message: Welcome to {community}, {name}! 👋 Please read the rules: {rules}. Never share your seed phrase — admins will never DM you first.
welcome._note: Sent to new members on join. Placeholders: {name} {community} {rules}. The member's name is sanitized (URLs stripped) before insertion. Set enabled=false to turn off.

moderation._note_bannedSubstrings: Matched as NORMALIZED substrings (examples/normalize.ts), never compiled as raw regex — avoids ReDoS.
moderation._note_massPingTokens: Channel-wide @ping tokens blocked from NON-admins: a non-admin who posts @<token> (e.g. @everyone) has the message removed (mass-ping signal). Admins are exempt via immunity/trust. Matched as ESCAPED literals at a real mention position (so name@everyone.com and @allan are NOT flagged) — never compiled as a raw regex (ReDoS-safe). Edit to add/remove terms; set to [] to disable entirely.

contentFilters._note: Combot 'Filters' parity — deterministic content-type filters applied by examples/content-filters.ts BEFORE the scam scorer. Each value is the action when a message carries that content type: allow | warn | delete | mute | kick | ban (or 'off' = disabled = allow). Order mirrors the Combot panel. NEW/FLAGGED members are additionally restricted by trust.newMemberNoLinks / newMemberNoMedia even when a filter says 'allow'.
contentFilters._note_wordsFilter: Action taken when a moderation.bannedSubstrings term matches. The word list itself lives in moderation.bannedSubstrings (normalized substrings, never raw regex).
contentFilters._note_links: Action for any non-whitelisted link. The domain allow/block lists are community.officialDomains / community.blocklistDomains — whitelisted links are never filtered.
contentFilters._note_mentions: Filters ANY @mention. Separate from the scorer's mass-mention signal (>=5 mentions), which always scores regardless of this filter.
contentFilters._note_guestMode: Member-state policy, not a per-message type: restrict unverified 'guest' members. Enforce via the trust state machine, not the message detector.
contentFilters._note_messageLength: Set action to delete/warn and max to a character count to cap long messages. 'off' disables it.

adjudication._note: When score is in [low,high], optionally call an LLM judge (examples/llm-adjudicator.ts). The message is passed as data, never as instructions.

enrichment._note: Resolve token mints / addresses via birdeye/helius/wallet-analysis and feed externalSignals (examples/enrich-token.ts).

auditLog._note: MEE6 'Audit Logging' parity (examples/audit-log.ts). Sends server events to `channel`. Toggle any event below (omit/true = on, false = off). ignoredChannels skips message edit/delete events in those channels. dontLogBots skips events whose ACTOR is a bot (your own auto-mod actions log as 'agent', not a bot). PRIVACY: logs metadata only — who/what/where/when — never deleted/edited message CONTENT (unlike MEE6), honoring privacy.storeRawMessages.

ticketing._note: MEE6 'Ticketing' parity (Discord). Core logic in examples/ticketing.ts; discord.js wiring in examples/discord/ticketing.ts. A panel button opens a private channel under `openCategory`; ticket managers run the /ticket-* commands; a transcript is built on close/delete. NOTE: transcripts intentionally contain message content (the record the opener asked for) — keep them to the transcript channel and respect retention.
ticketing._note_commands: Toggle the slash commands. MEE6 default: claim off, close/delete/reopen on. Only ticket managers can run them, inside a ticket channel.

panels.name: Superteam Suporte

panelMessage.title: Canal de Suporte
panelMessage.description: Este é o suporte da Superteam Brasil. Clique no botão para abrir seu ticket pessoal com o time de suporte.

panels.introMessage:
Seu ticket foi criado {opener} @Core Mods @Moderator.
Por favor forneça as informações relevantes para que possamos te ajudar melhor.

analytics._note: Combot 'Analytics' parity (examples/analytics.ts). Computed from join/leave/message EVENTS — member id + timestamp only, NEVER message content. tzOffsetMinutes sets the timezone for daily/heatmap bucketing (-180 = UTC-3 / BRT). Each metric is compared to the immediately preceding equal-length window. Keep raw events only retentionDays, then drop to aggregates.

directory._note: Member roster (examples/member-directory.ts) — Combot 'Users' table WITHOUT XP. Columns: ID, Name, Username, MSG (period), AD active-days, Warns, MSG(all), Last msg, Joined, Left, Lang, Trust. defaultFilter: current|all|left. defaultSort: name|msga|activeDays|warns|msgAll|lastMsg|joined|left. Surfaced in the bots as /members (Telegram) and !members (Discord), admin-only.

secrets._note: Bot token comes from env BOT_TOKEN / a secret manager. NEVER store tokens in this file or the repo.
